title: The Rock Goes Green — Solar Panel Design
tagline: Solar thermal system design for Brown’s Rockefeller Library
lede: Five-person thermodynamics final project: designing a solar thermal system to replace the 20 kW natural gas boiler at Brown's Rockefeller Library — "The Rock."
cover: ../The Rock Goes Green Solar Panel Design Project/Cover Photo/Screenshot 2026-07-14 at 2.43.20 PM.png

blocks.heading: Objective
body: The Rock's current 20 kW natural gas boiler runs at roughly 50% efficiency. Our goal was a solar thermal system that meets the library's ~87,600 kWh/year heating demand, cuts fossil fuel consumption, and pays for itself in a reasonable period.

blocks.heading: Design
body: The final design uses 25 Apricus ETC-30 evacuated tube collectors in a five-by-five series-parallel configuration on the south-facing roof. A 50% propylene glycol–water loop carries heat through a 55 ft² heat exchanger into a 6,263 L storage tank feeding the building's heating system. Using the HDKR insolation model with Providence TMY3 weather data, we found the optimum collector tilt: 35°, capturing 1,581 kWh/m² per year.

blocks.source: ../The Rock Goes Green Solar Panel Design Project/Project Photos/Screenshot 2026-07-14 at 2.44.04 PM.png
blocks.caption: Optimum tilt angle via the HDKR model — 35° yields 1,581 kWh/m²/yr

blocks.source: ../The Rock Goes Green Solar Panel Design Project/Project Photos/Screenshot 2026-07-14 at 2.44.40 PM.png
blocks.caption: Seasonal glycol inlet and outlet temperature model

blocks.source: ../The Rock Goes Green Solar Panel Design Project/Project Photos/Screenshot 2026-07-14 at 2.44.28 PM.png
blocks.caption: System sizing — storage tank, heat exchanger, and pumps

blocks.heading: Economics & Impact
body: The system costs about $64,180 and saves roughly $9,985 per year in natural gas — a 6.4-year payback — while avoiding ~31.6 metric tons of CO₂ emissions annually. Safety measures include relief valves, an expansion tank, freeze protection, and a biodegradable, food-safe working fluid.

blocks.source: ../The Rock Goes Green Solar Panel Design Project/Project Presentation/Thermo Final Presentation.pdf
